text.title: CQRS Pattern Decision Tree
text.summary: Routing tree from observable signal to a rule id in `01-core-rules.xml`. Every leaf resolves to either a rule id or `skip-this-methodology`.

decision-tree.root-question: Which observable signal best describes the current state of the artefact under review?

branch.question: Is the system simple CRUD with no behavioural domain logic?

conclusion.verdict: skip CQRS; the overhead has no payoff

branch.question: Are commands and queries sharing a model?

conclusion.verdict: split into command model + read-model DTOs

branch.question: Does the command return the persisted entity?

conclusion.verdict: return void or ID; clients query for the data
extract the side effect into a command

branch.question: Is the read model hand-updated instead of projected from events?

conclusion.verdict: build a projection consumer; rebuild from event log

branch.question: Is one read model serving wildly different queries?

conclusion.verdict: signal does not match; route to a different methodology or proceed without this one

section.title: Usage
section.p: Apply the tree top-to-bottom against the artefact (PR diff, config, design doc). The first matching branch is decisive; later branches are not evaluated. If multiple branches match, fix in the order listed — they typically depend on earlier fixes.
